text:
Once upon a time in a moonlit glen, there roamed a fox whose fur shimmered silver under the stars. This fox, Nightweaver, had an enchanted ability to glide silently through the night, listening deeply to the whispers of the universe.

One moonless summer night, Nightweaver heard a plaintive song coming from the clearing at the far end of the glen. It was the melody of a lost love, sweet yet melancholy.

Curiosity piqued, Nightweaver embarked on an adventure. As luck would have it, his keen senses detected a peculiar aura that shimmered softly around the trees. He followed the music into a clearing where an ancient oak stood, its branches spreading wide like an embracing grandmother.

Before him stood a figure draped in garments woven from starlight. She was young, only just beginning her journey as a bard, gifted with the voice that could soothe the deepest sorrows.

"Why do you humbly clutch your heart?" asked
